docs: https://persistiq.com/api-docs/index.html (live Swagger UI loading https://persistiq.com/api-docs/v1/swagger.json, a machine-readable OpenAPI 3.0.1 spec confirmed reachable and current during this review)
scope: Pass B full-surface expansion: contrary to this bundle's own prior review note (an earlier pass recorded persistiq.com/api-docs as dead/parked), a direct fetch of https://persistiq.com/api-docs/v1/swagger.json during THIS review returned a live, current, 14-path OpenAPI 3.0.1 spec -- the docs are reachable and this bundle is now grounded in that live spec rather than a secondary source. The spec confirms auth is an 'x-api-key' header (matching legacy's own 'X-API-KEY', an HTTP header name match being case-insensitive) against base_url https://api.persistiq.com. This bundle covers all 6 legacy-parity read streams (leads, users, campaigns, mailboxes, activities, accounts) UNCHANGED for parity, plus every additional real endpoint the live spec documents: 6 new read streams (dnc_domains, events, lead_fields, lead_statuses, tags, webhook_plugin) and 2 new fan-out sub-resource streams (campaign_leads, campaign_replies, both driven off the campaigns list). 7 write actions are added: update_lead is CORRECTED from this bundle's prior (guessed) PUT-with-title-field shape to the live spec's real PATCH /v1/leads/{id} with its real flat field set (status/status_id/owner_id/bounced/optedout/data); create_campaign, duplicate_campaign, add_lead_to_campaign, remove_lead_from_campaign, reply_to_campaign_message, and add_dnc_domain are new, each confirmed dialect-expressible (a flat top-level JSON body, or no body) directly from the live requestBody schema. create_lead (POST /v1/leads) remains excluded: its live schema requires a top-level 'lead' wrapper object ({"lead": {...}, "creator_id":..., "dup":...}), an envelope the WriteAction dialect cannot express -- this ENGINE_GAP is unchanged from the prior review, now confirmed directly against the live spec rather than a secondary source. IMPORTANT CAVEAT (documented in docs.md): /v1/mailboxes, /v1/activities, and /v1/accounts -- 3 of the 6 legacy-parity streams -- do NOT appear anywhere in this live 14-path spec; they may be undocumented-but-still-live endpoints, deprecated, or a legacy implementation detail that was never independently verified against a real spec. Per this migration's own ground rule ('legacy is ground truth over any doc' when the two conflict), these 3 streams are kept EXACTLY as legacy implements them (parity-preserving), not removed -- this is an honest limitation of this review's source material, not a silent behavior change.

excluded.reason: single-lead detail; identical record shape to the leads list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream

ENGINE_GAP: create-a-lead's live OpenAPI requestBody schema requires a top-level 'lead' wrapper object ({"lead": {...fields...}, "creator_id", "dup"}), confirmed directly from https://persistiq.com/api-docs/v1/swagger.json; the engine's declarative WriteAction dialect always sends the record's own fields directly (or a body_fields allow-list of top-level keys), with no envelope/wrapper mechanism, so this mutation is not currently dialect-expressible without silently diverging from the real request shape

repoints the account's outbound webhook-notification URLs (post_new_prospect_url, post_updated_prospect_url, raw_events_url, post_email_reply_url, post_email_opened_url) of the caller's choosing; deferred pending its own dedicated security review, consistent with this dialect's general caution around webhook-URL mutations elsewhere in this migration (persona, bitly, perk)
